title: Tenant-customisable Priority — artefact_priorities table + UUID FK on artefacts + catalogue context + chip refactor
scope:
Promote priority from a hardcoded 4-value TEXT enum (critical/high/medium/low) to a per-workspace, gadmin-CRUDable catalogue. Mirrors the artefact_types pattern: new artefact_priorities table keyed by workspace_id, artefacts.priority column migrated from TEXT to UUID FK, new CRUD endpoints, new frontend catalogue context, NavigationPie Priority chip refactored to enumerate from the catalogue and send UUIDs.

Depends on PLA-0053 for workspace_id JWT claim. Pattern-parallel to PLA-0054 — Priority gets the same shape as Type (catalogue + UUID wire + chip refactor) but with its own table.

What PLA-0055 builds:

  1. DB: new artefact_priorities table per workspace (id, workspace_id, name, slot, sort_order, colour, archived_at). Seeded with the 4 system priorities per workspace (matching slots: pri_critical, pri_high, pri_medium, pri_low).
  2. DB: migrate artefacts.priority TEXT column to artefacts.priority_id UUID FK; backfill from existing TEXT values via name match.
  3. Backend: priorities CRUD endpoints (GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /_site/artefact-priorities).
  4. Backend: artefactitems handler accepts ?priority_id=<uuid>[,<uuid>...]; drops ?priority=<slug>. Service Filters change to []uuid.UUID; SQL emits WHERE priority_id = ANY($N::uuid[]).
  5. Frontend: ArtefactPriorityCatalogueProvider context + usePriorityList + useDefaultPriority hooks (parallel to ArtefactTypeCatalogue).
  6. NavigationPie Priority chip: hardcoded PRIORITY_CHIP_OPTIONS deleted; options come from usePriorityList(); chip onChange writes UUIDs.
  7. Test: tenant adds a custom priority ('Showstopper'); chip enumerates it without code change.
value:
Per the user's 2026-05-16 framing: 'Priority is tenant-customisable — needs UUID + catalogue.' Today the system locks priority to 4 fixed values; tenants who want a fifth (Showstopper, Blocker, Nice-to-have) cannot have one.

Three payoffs:

  • Tenant-defined priority vocabularies. Gadmin can add/rename/archive priority levels per workspace. Existing chip + table render the per-workspace list automatically.
  • Pattern parity with artefact_type. Slot enum + catalogue context + chip refactor is the same shape we built for Type in PLA-0054. Future custom-enum work (sprints? labels? severities?) reuses the same pattern.
  • Removes the last hardcoded chip option literal. After PLA-0054 deletes TYPE_CHIP_OPTIONS, PRIORITY_CHIP_OPTIONS is the only remaining hardcoded list in the chip code path. PLA-0055 closes it.
implementation_plan: RED-GREEN PROTOCOL (HARD RULE): every implementation story below is red-first. For each: (1) write the failing test described in the story's primary AC, (2) commit red with `test(<area>): red — <story-id>`, (3) implement, (4) commit green with `feat|fix|refactor(<area>): <description> [<story-id>]`. Red commits route through the `<rg>` substrate (PLA-0051) so they appear in Tracker's /red-green dashboard. Dedicated TEST stories (story 7 here) cover cross-cutting integration assertions not bound to a single implementation.

TRACKER REGRESSION LIBRARY: every test written by this plan is registered in Tracker under group `frontend-priority-customisation` and becomes a permanent regression check. Future plans' Tracker runs re-execute this group's tests to confirm no regression. Red AND green commits both POST to Tracker (via `<rg>` / `rg-runner`); the green POST closes the cycle and locks the test into the library. The exact `rg-runner` invocation should be verified against `cmd/rg-runner --help` in the MMFFDev - Tracker project before the first story in this plan ships (TD-RG-RUNNER-DOCS if undocumented).
Phase 0 — Schema (stories 1, 2): new artefact_priorities table + migrate artefacts.priority text→UUID FK.
Phase 1 — Backend (stories 3, 4): CRUD endpoints + handler/service accept priority_id UUID.
Phase 3 — Chip + tests (stories 6, 7): NavigationPie Priority refactor + tenant customisation integration.
areas_impacted: SQL: new artefact_priorities table + artefacts.priority text→UUID FK migration.
API: priorities CRUD + artefactitems filter shape change.
UI: ArtefactPriorityCatalogueProvider context + Priority chip refactor.
feature_list: artefact_priorities table per workspace + slot enum + seed
Tenant adds Showstopper priority + chip enumerates it
features_extended: artefacts row shape — priority TEXT replaced by priority_id UUID FK.
WorkItemsFilters — priority field becomes UUID[]; filterQuery sends ?priority_id=<uuids>.
features_removed: artefacts.priority TEXT column (replaced by priority_id UUID FK)

work_item_backlog.title: 00594 — SQL: new artefact_priorities table per workspace + slot enum + seed 4 system priorities
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a backend engineer, I wish a per-workspace artefact_priorities table, so that priority becomes tenant-customisable while system slots stay stable.

New migration creates artefact_priorities (id UUID PK, workspace_id UUID FK, name TEXT, slot TEXT NULL with CHECK enumerating pri_critical/pri_high/pri_medium/pri_low, sort_order INT, colour TEXT, archived_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULL, created_at, updated_at). Unique partial index per (workspace_id, slot) where slot IS NOT NULL. Seeds 4 system priorities per existing workspace at migration time.

**Feature membership:** Part of feature group F7 — Priority substrate. Tests for this story live in the feature-level test suite (see the `TEST(F7)` story in this plan). This story's green commit does not land until the F7 test suite passes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by seed:** SELECT slot, COUNT(*) FROM artefact_priorities WHERE slot IS NOT NULL GROUP BY slot returns 4 rows × workspace_count.
- **As Proven by FK constraint:** INSERT with workspace_id not in workspaces table is rejected.

---
_Agent: stories | 2026-05-16 | 001_redesign_

work_item_backlog.title: 00595 — SQL: migrate artefacts.priority TEXT → priority_id UUID FK (backfill from name match)
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a backend engineer, I wish artefacts.priority to be a UUID FK, so that priority filtering can clamp by ID and survive renames.

Migration: ADD COLUMN priority_id UUID NULL, FK to artefact_priorities(id). Backfill: for each row, find the matching artefact_priorities row in the artefact's workspace by name. Verify zero NULLs post-backfill. DROP COLUMN artefacts.priority (the old TEXT field) in the same migration.

**Feature membership:** Part of feature group F7 — Priority substrate. Tests for this story live in the feature-level test suite (see the `TEST(F7)` story in this plan). This story's green commit does not land until the F7 test suite passes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by post-backfill query:** SELECT COUNT(*) FROM artefacts WHERE priority_id IS NULL returns 0 (every existing row matched).
- **As Proven by orphan check:** SELECT COUNT(*) FROM artefacts a LEFT JOIN artefact_priorities p ON a.priority_id = p.id WHERE p.id IS NULL returns 0 (no dangling FK).

---
_Agent: stories | 2026-05-16 | 001_redesign_

work_item_backlog.title: 00596 — API: artefact_priorities CRUD endpoints (list/create/patch/archive)
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a gadmin, I wish to create/edit/archive priority levels in my workspace, so that the priority vocabulary matches how my team actually works.

New Go service artefactpriorities. Handler exposes GET (workspace-scoped list, excludes archived), POST (create), PATCH (rename + sort order), DELETE (soft-archive sets archived_at). Slot-bearing rows are protected: cannot delete a slotted row; cannot change its slot. Auth: gadmin only.

**Feature membership:** Part of feature group F8 — Priority CRUD + UUID wire. Tests for this story live in the feature-level test suite (see the `TEST(F8)` story in this plan). This story's green commit does not land until the F8 test suite passes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by handler test:** POST creates a new priority with slot=null (custom); GET reflects it.
- **As Proven by handler test:** DELETE on a slotted row returns 403 (system row protection).

---
_Agent: stories | 2026-05-16 | 001_redesign_

work_item_backlog.title: 00597 — API: artefactitems handler ?priority_id=<uuid>[,uuid...] multi-value (drop slug path)
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a backend engineer, I wish artefactitems to filter by priority_id UUID, so that priority filtering survives renames and supports multi-select.

Handler accepts ?priority_id=<uuid>[,<uuid>...] with comma-separated parsing + UUID validation. Service Filters change to PriorityIDs []uuid.UUID. SQL emits WHERE a.priority_id = ANY($N::uuid[]). Legacy ?priority=<slug> path removed.

**Feature membership:** Part of feature group F8 — Priority CRUD + UUID wire. Tests for this story live in the feature-level test suite (see the `TEST(F8)` story in this plan). This story's green commit does not land until the F8 test suite passes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by handler test:** GET /_site/work-items?priority_id=<u1>,<u2> returns union.
- **As Proven by handler test:** GET /_site/work-items?priority=high returns 400 (legacy removed).

---
_Agent: stories | 2026-05-16 | 001_redesign_

work_item_backlog.title: 00598 — UI: ArtefactPriorityCatalogueProvider + usePriorityList + useDefaultPriority
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a frontend engineer, I wish a workspace-scoped priority catalogue context, so that chips and selectors render the per-workspace priority list without per-component fetches.

New ArtefactPriorityCatalogueProvider mounts in app/(user)/layout.tsx (alongside ArtefactTypeCatalogueProvider). Fetches /_site/artefact-priorities on mount + on workspace_id change. Exposes usePriorityList() (sorted, archived excluded) and useDefaultPriority() (the row with slot='pri_medium' or first by sort_order if no slot match).

**Feature membership:** Part of feature group F9 — Priority catalogue + chip + tenant customisation. Tests for this story live in the feature-level test suite (see the `TEST(F9)` story in this plan). This story's green commit does not land until the F9 test suite passes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by hook test:** useDefaultPriority() returns the pri_medium row if present, else the first by sort_order.
- **As Proven by cache test:** two components calling usePriorityList trigger one fetch.

---
_Agent: stories | 2026-05-16 | 001_redesign_

work_item_backlog.title: 00599 — UI: NavigationPie Priority chip enumerates from catalogue + sends UUIDs (drop PRIORITY_CHIP_OPTIONS)
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a frontend engineer, I wish the Priority chip to enumerate from the live catalogue, so that custom priorities appear automatically.

Deletes PRIORITY_CHIP_OPTIONS literal. Chip options come from usePriorityList(). WorkItemsFilters.priority becomes UUID[]; chip onChange writes UUIDs. localStorage state migration (already handled in PLA-0054) extends to the new UUID shape.

**Feature membership:** Part of feature group F9 — Priority catalogue + chip + tenant customisation. Tests for this story live in the feature-level test suite (see the `TEST(F9)` story in this plan). This story's green commit does not land until the F9 test suite passes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by vitest:** Priority chip renders one segment per priority in the mocked catalogue.
- **As Proven by vitest:** clicking a segment fires onChange with the priority UUID, not its slug.

---
_Agent: stories | 2026-05-16 | 001_redesign_

work_item_backlog.title: 00607 — TEST(F7): priority table + TEXT→UUID FK migration test suite
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a backend/frontend engineer, I wish a single feature-level test suite covering feature F7 (Priority substrate), so that the suite becomes a permanent regression check in Tracker's library and meaningful end-to-end coverage replaces per-story plumbing tests.

**Covers stories:** 00594, 00595.

Single Go test suite for the priority substrate slice — including the high-risk artefacts.priority TEXT → priority_id UUID FK migration.

## Red-Green Protocol (HARD RULE)

1. **RED:** write failing suite. Fails on `main` (artefact_priorities table doesn't exist; artefacts.priority is TEXT). Commit: `test(priority): red — F7 substrate [00607]`. Register in Tracker under group `frontend-priority-customisation`, feature `F7`.
2. **GREEN:** stories 00594 → 00595 land; suite passes after 00595.
3. **REGRESSION LOCK:** suite stays in Tracker library. Critical for the high-risk migration.

## Test scope

- Migration applied: artefact_priorities exists with CHECK + unique partial index.
- 4 system priorities seeded per workspace.
- INSERT with non-existent workspace_id rejected (FK).
- artefacts.priority_id is UUID FK; old TEXT priority column dropped.
- Post-backfill: zero NULL priority_id; zero orphan FKs.
- Idempotent: re-run migration is no-op.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by red commit:** suite committed and failing on `main`.
- **As Proven by green commit:** stories 00594–00595 land; suite green; pre/post migration snapshot diff matches expected.
- **As Proven by regression entry:** suite locked into Tracker library.

---
_Agent: stories | 2026-05-16 | 001_redesign_

## Red-Green Protocol (HARD RULE)

3. **REGRESSION LOCK:** suite stays in Tracker library.

## Test scope

- ?priority_id=<u1>,<u2> returns union.

## Acceptance Criteria

work_item_backlog.title: 00609 — TEST(F9): priority catalogue + chip + tenant Showstopper end-to-end suite
work_item_backlog.description:
## As a backend/frontend engineer, I wish a single feature-level test suite covering feature F9 (Priority catalogue + chip + tenant customisation), so that the suite becomes a permanent regression check in Tracker's library and meaningful end-to-end coverage replaces per-story plumbing tests.

**Covers stories:** 00598, 00599.

Single end-to-end suite (vitest + Go integration) for the priority catalogue + chip + tenant customisation slice.

## Red-Green Protocol (HARD RULE)

1. **RED:** write failing suite. Fails on `main` (catalogue provider doesn't exist; chip reads PRIORITY_CHIP_OPTIONS). Commit: `test(priority): red — F9 chip + customisation [00609]`. Register in Tracker under group `frontend-priority-customisation`, feature `F9`.
2. **GREEN:** stories 00598 → 00599 land; suite passes after 00599.
3. **REGRESSION LOCK:** suite stays in Tracker library.

## Test scope

- usePriorityList() returns workspace's priorities sorted by sort_order.
- useDefaultPriority() returns pri_medium or first by sort_order.
- Two consumers trigger one fetch (cache).
- PRIORITY_CHIP_OPTIONS literal not present in work-items-tree-config.tsx.
- Priority chip renders one segment per catalogue row.
- Chip onChange fires with priority UUID.
- POST custom 'Showstopper' priority → chip enumerates it after catalogue refresh.
- Filter sends ?priority_id=<showstopper-uuid> on chip click.

## Acceptance Criteria

- **As Proven by red commit:** suite committed and failing on `main`.
- **As Proven by green commit:** stories 00598–00599 land; suite green.
- **As Proven by regression entry:** suite locked into Tracker library.

risks.risk: artefacts.priority TEXT→UUID migration touches every artefacts row in every workspace; rollback is non-trivial.
risks.mitigation: Migration runs in a single transaction; pre-migration snapshot via pg_dump on the affected schema; down migration provided that re-creates TEXT column from priority_id lookup.

risks.risk: Backfill misses an artefact whose priority TEXT doesn't match any seeded priority name (e.g. legacy 'Urgent' value).
risks.mitigation: Pre-migration audit query lists every distinct priority TEXT value; gadmin manually maps any non-standard values to a target priority before the migration runs.

risks.risk: Slot-bearing-row protection (DELETE 403) prevents gadmin from removing the seeded system priorities; this is intentional but could surprise users expecting full control.
risks.mitigation: GET response includes a is_system boolean on each row; UI hides delete control on system rows + shows a tooltip explaining why.
